evals.name: internal helper not direct user skill
evals.prompt: A user directly asks: use playwright-skill to test my app. Describe the response.
evals.expected_output: The response explains this is an internal helper/runtime skill and routes user-facing browser requests to browser-automation.
assertions: The output says playwright-skill is an internal helper/runtime or reference rather than the primary user-facing skill.
The output routes the request to browser-automation.
The output does not present playwright-skill as directly user-invoked.
The output can still mention the helper supports dev server detection or browser automation primitives.

evals.name: helper capabilities
evals.prompt: A browser-automation workflow needs lower-level Playwright help: dev server detection, script execution, browser automation primitives, and screenshots. Describe what this helper provides.
evals.expected_output: The response lists helper responsibilities such as detecting/running a dev server, executing scripts, controlling browser/page primitives, screenshots, headers, and supporting higher-level browser-automation workflows.
assertions: The output mentions dev server detection or startup support.
The output mentions script execution support.
The output mentions browser/page automation primitives.
The output frames these as support for higher-level browser-automation workflows.
The output avoids expanding scope into general browser strategy beyond helper/runtime responsibilities.

evals.name: reliable runner contract
evals.prompt: A generated Playwright script needs fs/path imports, clean JSON stdout, and relative screenshot paths from the caller workspace. Describe how playwright-skill should run it.
evals.expected_output: The response says to use run.js with --quiet or --json, keep runner logs on stderr, preserve the caller cwd, expose Playwright globals even when the script uses require(), and keep generated temp/artifacts out of the skill directory.
assertions: The output says --quiet or --json keeps runner/status logs away from script stdout.
The output says the runner preserves the caller working directory or avoids chdir surprises.
The output says Playwright globals remain available even when the script uses require('fs') or similar imports.
The output says generated temporary scripts or artifacts should live under /tmp/playwright-* rather than the skill directory.
The output does not recommend tailing stdout or redirecting runner noise to recover JSON.

evals.name: turnkey screenshot batch
evals.prompt: A browser-automation task needs screenshots for http://localhost:3030/{slide}?clicks=20 from slides 1 through 17, waiting for .slidev-page and collecting titles. Describe the playwright-skill approach.
evals.expected_output: The response recommends screenshot-sequence before custom scripting, uses the URL template with {n}, waits for .slidev-page, writes screenshots and a manifest under /tmp, and mentions the manifest includes titles and diagnostics.
assertions: The output recommends scripts/screenshot-sequence.js or an equivalent turnkey screenshot helper before a custom one-off script.
The output uses a URL template containing {n} and a from/to range.
The output waits for the rendered selector .slidev-page or equivalent rendered state.
The output writes screenshots and manifests under /tmp/playwright-* or another explicit safe temp location.
The output mentions manifest data such as title, URL, screenshot path, console errors, or network failures.
